what_it_does: Defines a systemd service unit that launches /usr/local/bin/llama-swap with a fixed config file at /home/tim/llama-swap/config.yaml, binds to 127.0.0.1:9090, enables config-file watching, restarts on failure, and logs output to the systemd journal.
observations: ExecStart uses an absolute path /usr/local/bin/llama-swap with flags -config, -listen, and -watch-config.
The config path is /home/tim/llama-swap/config.yaml, a user home directory.
The service listens only on the loopback interface at port 9090.
Both stdout and stderr are sent to the journal.
standouts: Hardcoded /home/tim path inside a system service unit.
Description explicitly marks the service as '(test)'.
-watch-config flag implies dynamic reloading without a full service restart.
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit section layout [Unit]/[Service]/[Install].
Uses absolute binary path and journal logging.
Breaks the convention of avoiding personal home directories in shared service units.
Breaks the convention of stable service descriptions by including '(test)'.
concerns: Hardcoded /home/tim path may fail or be inappropriate outside that user's environment.
No User= directive is present, so the service may run as root if installed system-wide.
Description labels the unit as a test, which is unusual for an operational service path.
No WorkingDirectory, Environment, or ExecReload directives are present.
notes: This is a systemd wrapper for a local llama-swap instance, currently tagged as a test and tied to a specific user's home directory.
questions: Is this intended as a system-wide service or a user service?
Who is 'tim' and is the /home/tim path portable?
Why is the unit description marked '(test)'?
Does llama-swap require any environment variables or a specific working directory?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a llama-swap model swapper for testing; the actual directives run llama-swap with config watching and a localhost listener, which aligns except for the test qualifier.
intention_signals: The unit appears intended to keep a llama-swap daemon running continuously on the local machine, reloading its model configuration automatically and restarting if it crashes.
novelty: The -watch-config flag is the most distinctive detail, suggesting hot config reload; otherwise it is a conventional systemd service wrapper.
contribution: It records the runtime invocation, restart policy, logging target, and network binding for llama-swap, preserving the operational contract needed to run or reproduce the service.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that runs /usr/local/bin/llama-swap with /home/tim/llama-swap/config.yaml on 127.0.0.1:9090, with config watching, on-failure restart, and journal logging.
intention_for_embedding: To deploy llama-swap as a persistent local daemon that can reload its model configuration and recover from failures.
